Code fragment: public final TableField<ItemRecord, UUID> ID = createField(DSL.name("id"), SQLDataType.UUID.nullable(false), this, "");
Explanation: The primary key column is generically named 'id' instead of a more descriptive name like 'item_id'. Consider renaming the primary key to 'item_id' to make it self-documenting and easier to identify in queries involving multiple tables.

Code fragment: public final TableField<ItemRecord, Double> PRICE = createField(DSL.name("price"), SQLDataType.DOUBLE.nullable(false), this, "");
Explanation: The 'price' column uses DOUBLE which can cause rounding errors when storing monetary values. Use DECIMAL or NUMERIC with appropriate precision (e.g., DECIMAL(10,2)) to ensure accurate financial calculations.
